title: Inbox Triage Protocol
instruction: Process five inbox items exactly once. Star and label 'Finance Review' on the invoice that requires approval (not the cover sheet or remittance note). Archive the promotional offer. Forward the original security alert (not the follow-up saying it was already resolved) to sec-escalations@webagentbench.test. Label the travel request about the leadership offsite (not the dinner invitation) with 'Travel Follow-up'. Reply to the onboarding checklist email with the exact phrase 'I have completed the onboarding checklist.'.
eval_check_descriptions: Invoice email was starred and labeled correctly
Promotion email was archived out of the inbox
Security alert was forwarded to the escalation address
Travel request received the correct follow-up label
Onboarding message received the requested confirmation reply

resolved_targets.confirmation_phrase: I have completed the onboarding checklist.

emails.subject: Security alert follow-up: Calendar Bridge token already revoked
emails.body:
Following up on the earlier Calendar Bridge token alert. The token has already been revoked on our side and the issuing device was removed from the allow list.

No additional incident routing is needed unless the device reappears in the audit log.

Thanks,
Security Operations

emails.subject: Security alert: suspicious OAuth token
emails.body:
A new OAuth token labeled 'Calendar Bridge' was issued from an unrecognized macOS device at 03:14 UTC.

We have not revoked it yet because the workflow owner may still confirm it as valid, but the activity should go through the normal incident path if it is unexpected.

Thanks,
Security Operations

emails.subject: Revised budget worksheet
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Weekend bundle discount ends tonight
emails.body:
This weekend only: bundle pricing on office seating, monitor arms, and conference room accessories.

The storefront discount expires at midnight and includes free shipping on any order above the corporate minimum.

Thanks,
Northwind Labs

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
Sending a quick status update from today's working session.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Invoice 8842 cover sheet and remittance note
emails.body:
Sharing the remittance note and cover sheet for invoice 8842 before we close the weekly vendor packet.

No approval needed on this one yet. I mainly wanted you to have the support file in case finance asks for the remittance address.

Thanks,
Theo

emails.subject: Invoice 8842 needs approval
emails.body:
Attached is invoice 8842 covering the Denver freight overage, badge reprints, and on-site support from last week's event.

Accounts payable asked whether you can review it before Friday so the vendor stays on normal payment terms.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Priya

emails.subject: Travel request for Denver leadership dinner
emails.body:
Can you confirm whether the Denver dinner guest list should use the same travel code as the leadership offsite?

The restaurant needs the final count before I send the transportation confirmation.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Agenda draft for Monday check-in
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Updated rollout checklist
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Reminder on document review timing
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Travel request for Denver leadership offsite
emails.body:
Can you confirm the hotel block and flight cap for next week's Denver leadership offsite?

The venue needs the rooming list on Monday, and finance asked whether ground transportation should stay under the same trip code.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Complete your onboarding checklist
emails.body:
Reply once your laptop setup, MFA enrollment, payroll forms, and badge pickup are all complete so I can close the onboarding ticket.

If anything is still blocked, include the missing item in your response.

Thanks,
People Operations

emails.subject: Agenda draft for Monday check-in
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Elena
